items.headline: Spurs use complete effort to dominate Timberwolves, advance to WCF
items.blurb: Spurs use complete effort to dominate Timberwolves, advance to WCF — Answering the call to close out their second-round series, the San Antonio Spurs dominated the Minnesota Timberwolves for a wire-to-wire win on Friday night.

items.headline: Spurs eliminate Timberwolves, advance to Western Conference Finals vs. Thunder
items.blurb: Spurs eliminate Timberwolves, advance to Western Conference Finals vs. Thunder — The San Antonio Spurs are off to the Western Conference Finals for the first time since 2017.

items.headline: Winners, losers from NBA playoffs: Spurs, Stephon Castle sink Timberwolves
items.blurb: Winners, losers from NBA playoffs: Spurs, Stephon Castle sink Timberwolves — On a Friday night with two huge Game 6 battles, the Detroit Pistons avoided elimination by out-shooting the Cleveland Cavaliers, while the San Antonio Spurs blew out the Minnesota Timberwolves to earn a date with the Oklahoma City Thunder in the Western Conference Finals.

items.headline: Pistons' Cade Cunningham excited to return to Detroit for Game 7 vs. Cavaliers
items.blurb: Pistons' Cade Cunningham excited to return to Detroit for Game 7 vs. Cavaliers — The Detroit Pistons have officially forced a Game 7 against the Cleveland Cavaliers in the Eastern Conference semifinals after the team's Friday win, 115-94, on the road.

items.headline: Jalen Williams Ready For West Finals After Missing Six Games With Hamstring Strain
items.blurb: Jalen Williams Ready For West Finals After Missing Six Games With Hamstring Strain — Jalen Williams declared himself fully healthy for the Western Conference finals Friday, ending uncertainty over the Oklahoma City Thunder wing's availability after a six-game absence. Williams sustained a Grade 1 left hamstring strain during the second round against the Phoenix Suns. Williams addressed his status

items.headline: Warriors To Open Stephen Curry Extension Talks, Committed To Him Finishing Career With Franchise
items.blurb: Warriors To Open Stephen Curry Extension Talks, Committed To Him Finishing Career With Franchise — The Golden State Warriors plan to open contract extension discussions with Steph Curry this summer, general manager Mike Dunleavy confirmed Friday. items.headline: Warriors Expect Draymond Green To Return But Defer To His Decision
items.blurb: Warriors Expect Draymond Green To Return But Defer To His Decision — The Golden State Warriors want Draymond Green back, but the decision rests entirely with the veteran forward. General manager Mike Dunleavy made clear Friday that the organization has expressed its preference while leaving the choice to Green. items.headline: Warriors Expected To Keep 11th Pick To Add Young Talent To Aging Roster
items.blurb: Warriors Expected To Keep 11th Pick To Add Young Talent To Aging Roster — The Golden State Warriors front office is leaning toward keeping its 11th overall pick in an upcoming NBA draft widely considered strong through its lottery portion, sources told ESPN. The Warriors, carrying one of the oldest rosters in the league, are focused on adding younger talent to reinforce their depth. General manage

items.headline: Steve Kerr 'Thrilled' To Continue Coaching, Vows 'To Be Better' Next Season
items.blurb: Steve Kerr 'Thrilled' To Continue Coaching, Vows 'To Be Better' Next Season — Steve Kerr will coach the Golden State Warriors for a 13th season after a period of deliberation that included an honest personal evaluation. Kerr, who has won four NBA championships as a head coach, openly admitted his work this past season fell short of his own standards. "I know I have to be better," said Kerr. "I did

items.headline: Anunoby full participant at Knicks practice after hamstring injury
items.blurb: Anunoby full participant at Knicks practice after hamstring injury — OG Anunoby fully practised with the New York Knicks on Friday for the first time since injuring his hamstring, moving them closer to having their starting lineup intact for the Eastern Conference finals.

items.headline: Pistons roll past Cavaliers to force decisive Game 7
items.blurb: Pistons roll past Cavaliers to force decisive Game 7 — The Detroit Pistons aren’t done just yet.

items.headline: Bench players carry Pistons to Game 6 win over Cavaliers
items.blurb: Bench players carry Pistons to Game 6 win over Cavaliers — Cade Cunningham has carried the Detroit Pistons all season. With that season on the verge of ending, the Pistons' bench came through in a big way in their 115-94 Game 6 win over the Cleveland Cavaliers to extend their time in the NBA playoffs.

items.headline: Pistons’ hustle sequence ends with Reed’s tough and-one vs. Cavaliers
items.blurb: Pistons’ hustle sequence ends with Reed’s tough and-one vs. Cavaliers — After a missed three nearly sailed out of bounds, the Detroit Pistons somehow kept the possession alive before Ausar Thompson found Paul Reed for a strong and-one finish to cap an incredible hustle sequence.

items.headline: Pistons’ Thompson skies for incredible two-handed block of Strus attempt
items.blurb: Pistons’ Thompson skies for incredible two-handed block of Strus attempt — Detroit Pistons star defender Ausar Thompson tracks the Cleveland Cavaliers’ Max Strus in transition and elevates for an unbelievable two-handed block during the third quarter of Game 6.

items.headline: Cavaliers’ Harden enters top 10 in all-time playoff scoring with three
items.blurb: Cavaliers’ Harden enters top 10 in all-time playoff scoring with three — Watch as Cleveland Cavaliers guard James Harden hits his signature step-back three-pointer to officially move into the top 10 on the NBA’s all-time playoff scoring list.

items.headline: Steve Kerr remains committed to Warriors following doubts after season
items.blurb: Steve Kerr remains committed to Warriors following doubts after season — Once Steve Kerr came to the realization that there was nothing he’d rather do than coach the Golden State Warriors and the team figured out there was no one better to guide the organization through Stephen Curry’s final seasons, the final decision for both sides was easy.

items.headline: Mike Brown ‘coaching to win’ as Knicks prepare for Eastern Conference finals
items.blurb: Mike Brown ‘coaching to win’ as Knicks prepare for Eastern Conference finals — The New York Knicks already had a coach who could take them to the Eastern Conference finals — and they fired him immediately after.

items.blurb: NBA Executives Increasingly Expect Giannis Antetokounmpo Trade As Bucks Signal Desire To Move On — After years of speculation, the belief around the NBA has shifted with the expectation that the Milwaukee Bucks will trade Giannis Antetokounmpo this offseason. items.blurb: Executive Says Kawhi Leonard May Be Better Trade Target Than Giannis Antetokounmpo — While Giannis Antetokounmpo dominates offseason trade discussion , at least one NBA executive believes the Los Angeles Clippers hold an equally compelling asset in Kawhi Leonard. items.blurb: LeBron James Staying With Lakers Considered Most Likely As Market Offers Few Viable Other Options — LeBron James faces a narrow free agency market this offseason, with league insiders widely expecting him to return to the Los Angeles Lakers as much due to the circumstances as mutual desire between the two parties. items.headline: Myles Turner Reveals Lack Of Discipline Under Doc Rivers, Singles Out Giannis For Chronic Tardiness
items.blurb: Myles Turner Reveals Lack Of Discipline Under Doc Rivers, Singles Out Giannis For Chronic Tardiness — Milwaukee Bucks center Myles Turner publicly criticized the team's lack of discipline under former coach Doc Rivers, singling out Giannis Antetokounmpo for habitual tardiness during their disappointing 32-50 season. items.headline: Why Timberwolves need another star besides Anthony Edwards
items.blurb: Why Timberwolves need another star besides Anthony Edwards — The Minnesota Timberwolves are fresh off a 126-97 beatdown by the second-seeded San Antonio Spurs in the Western Conference semifinals. Although star guard Anthony Edwards and the Timberwolves have given the Spurs problems throughout the series, a much bigger issue has become evident for the Western Conference's sixth seed.

items.headline: Why Steve Kerr backtracked on retirement decision to stay with Warriors
items.blurb: Why Steve Kerr backtracked on retirement decision to stay with Warriors — Golden State Warriors coach Steve Kerr will be back next season, but the story certainly could have been much different.

items.blurb: Jalen Duren's postseason might cost him serious money — The Detroit Pistons center has struggled mightily in the playoffs, putting up single-digit scoring numbers in half of his team's games.

items.blurb: Dillon Brooks adds fuel to LeBron James feud with Michael Jordan comments — Brooks is once again fueling the flames of the idea that he hasn't let go of this beef against James.

items.headline: Victor Wembanyama: Scores 19 points in Game 6 win
items.blurb: Victor Wembanyama: Scores 19 points in Game 6 win — Wembanyama finished Friday's 139-109 win over the Timberwolves in Game 6 of the Western Conference Semifinals with 19 points (6-11 FG, 0-2 3Pt, 7-9 FT), six rebounds, three blocks and two assists across 27 minutes. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.headline: Anthony Edwards: Unable to lead Minnesota to Game 7
items.blurb: Anthony Edwards: Unable to lead Minnesota to Game 7 — Edwards finished with 24 points (9-26 FG, 2-7 3Pt, 4-7 FT), two rebounds, two assists and three steals across 36 minutes in Friday's 139-109 loss to San Antonio in Game 6 of the Western Conference Semifinals. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.headline: Cavaliers taking in early money, with chance to eliminate top-seeded Pistons
items.blurb: Cavaliers taking in early money, with chance to eliminate top-seeded Pistons — The Borgata sports director Thomas Gable joins VSiN By The Books to discuss where the money is going for Game 6 in both the Cavaliers vs. Pistons and Spurs vs. Timberwolves matchups, and where you should lay your bets.

items.headline: Stephon Castle: Stuffs stat sheet in victory
items.blurb: Stephon Castle: Stuffs stat sheet in victory — Castle contributed 32 points (11-16 FG, 5-7 3Pt, 5-6 FT), 11 rebounds and six assists across 30 minutes in Friday's 139-109 Game 6 win over the Timberwolves in the Western Conference Semifinals. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.headline: Analyst urges Timberwolves to make blockbuster trade to help Anthony Edwards
items.blurb: Analyst urges Timberwolves to make blockbuster trade to help Anthony Edwards — Anthony Edwards and the Minnesota Timberwolves are pushing hard against Victor Wembanyama and the San Antonio Spurs in the Western Conference semifinals, but find themselves down 3-2 in the series heading into their matchup on Friday.

items.headline: James Harden leads Cavaliers to Game 5 OT win over Pistons on monumental night
items.blurb: James Harden leads Cavaliers to Game 5 OT win over Pistons on monumental night — Midseason trade acquisition James Harden was instrumental to Cleveland's Game 5 success on Wednesday.

items.blurb: Bryce Cotton To Play For Australia In World Cup Qualifiers — Bryce Cotton has spent years playing professionally in Australia. Now, Cotton will suit up for Australia's national team for FIBA World Cup qualifiers this summer. Cotten became a naturalized citizen of Australia last fall. items.headline: De'Aaron Fox: Returns to Game 6
items.blurb: De'Aaron Fox: Returns to Game 6 — Fox (ankle) started the second half of Friday's Game 6 of the Western Conference Semifinals versus the Timberwolves, Paul Garcia of ProjectSpurs.com reports. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.headline: De'Aaron Fox: Heads to locker room Friday
items.blurb: De'Aaron Fox: Heads to locker room Friday — Fox (ankle) exited to the locker room in the second quarter of Friday's Game 6 of the Western Conference Semifinals against Minnesota, Michael C. Wright of ESPN.com reports. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.headline: Duncan Robinson Upgraded To Questionable For Game 6
items.blurb: Duncan Robinson Upgraded To Questionable For Game 6 — The Detroit Pistons upgraded Duncan Robinson to questionable for Friday's Game 6 against the Cleveland Cavaliers. Kevin Huerter and Caris LeVert are also questionable with a left adductor strain and a right heel contusion respectively. Robinson, who missed Game 5, is averaging 11.5 points and 2.5 assists in the playoffs.

items.headline: Jazz Have Reached Out To Wizards About Trading For No. 1 Pick
items.blurb: Jazz Have Reached Out To Wizards About Trading For No. 1 Pick — The Utah Jazz have reached out to the Washington Wizards over potentially trading for the No. 1 pick in the upcoming NBA Draft, sources told Marc J. items.blurb: Charles Barkley steals the show with Max Strus line after Cavs beat Pistons in Game 5 — Barkley just couldn't help but move away from the serious talk to say something that perhaps people were thinking but not saying out loud.

items.blurb: Terry Stotts, Jerry Stackhouse Leaving Warriors; Willie Green Among Candidates For Assistant Job — The Golden State Warriors are losing lassistant Terry Stotts and defensive coordinator Jerry Stackhouse this offseason.
